text:
Once upon a time, in a small village nestled between rolling hills and a serene lake, lived a man named Bartholomew. Bartholomew spent his days tending to his garden and his books. His garden was an extraordinary place. It flourished with roses so red they nearly glowed, tulips that could sing delightful little songs, and beans that produced beans that danced when tapped.

People traveled far to visit Bartholomew. He was a recluse, but his garden was his paradise. One sunny morning, as he tended his morning glances, he noticed something unusual. A pink feather blew lightly onto the path, catching his eye.

Excited, Bartholomew followed the feather. It led him to a small cottage by the lake. The door was ajar, and inside sat a girl with gentle brown eyes and long, flowing hair. She was playing a melody on a flute.
